Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer using the SilaCalc platform.

name.description: The full name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer.

address.description: The physical address of the customer.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a construction project created by a customer.

projectDescription.description: A brief description of the project.

createdAt.description: The date and time the project was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time the project was last updated.

Room.title: Room
Room.description: Represents a room within a project, including its dimensions and calculated material requirements.

name.description: The name of the room (e.g., Living Room, Bedroom 1).

length.description: The length of the room in meters.

width.description: The width of the room in meters.

blocks.description: Number of blocks required for the room.

beamsLength.description: Total length of beams required for the room in meters.

concreteVolume.description: Volume of concrete required for the room in cubic meters.

MaterialCost.title: MaterialCost
MaterialCost.description: Stores the cost of different materials used in the project, potentially varying by region.

id.description: Unique identifier for the material cost record.

region.description: Geographic region for which the material cost is applicable.

concreteCost.description: Cost per cubic meter of concrete.

steelCost.description: Cost per unit of steel (e.g., per meter or kg).

definition.description: Stores customer data.  Secured by user ID. `customerId` MUST match `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer, which must match the user's UID.

definition.description: Stores project data for a specific customer. Includes `customerId` to enforce ownership.  Secured such that the `customerId` MUST match `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer, which must match the user's UID.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

definition.description: Stores room data for a specific project and customer. Includes `customerId` and `projectId` to enforce ownership. Secured such that the `customerId` MUST match `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer, which must match the user's UID.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

params.name: roomId
params.description: The unique identifier for the room.

definition.description: Stores material cost data, potentially varying by region. Secured such that only authenticated user can read and write data.

params.name: materialCostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the material cost record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to store customer, project, room, and material cost data for the SilaCalc platform. It follows the principle of Authorization Independence by storing customerId within each project, enabling direct access control without requiring parent document reads. The structure prioritizes path-based ownership for user-specific data (customers and their projects) and segregation of material cost data, which are potentially global or region-specific.

The chosen structure enables secure list operations (QAPs) by:

1.  Segregating customer data under `/customers/{customerId}` enabling simple rules to secure customer data to the customer with the matching `request.auth.uid`.
2.  Placing project data under `/customers/{customerId}/projects/{projectId}`, ensuring projects are only listed in the scope of user-owned customer data. Rules can secure project access to the customer with the matching `request.auth.uid`.
3.  Placing room data under `/customers/{customerId}/projects/{projectId}/rooms/{roomId}`, ensuring rooms are only listed in the scope of user-owned customer/project data. Rules can secure room access to the customer with the matching `request.auth.uid`.
4. Material costs are stored in a single collection allowing simple rules to secure material cost to the authenticated user
